probes.name: service-account-db-writable
probes.description: The `service_accounts` table is reachable AND writable. Performs a no-op INSERT + ROLLBACK inside a transaction to verify DDL / DML privileges. Read-only DB aborts boot because rotation + disable writes would fail silently at runtime.

probes.name: service-account-schema-current
probes.description: The `service_accounts` table has every column the module expects (id, application_id, tenant_id, name, description, is_enabled, signer_kid, secret_hash, secret_rotated_at, expires_at, last_used_at, last_used_ip, status, metadata, audit + timestamps). Catches unmigrated environments before they crash on first exchange.

probes.name: service-account-hs256-key-configured
probes.description: At least one enabled row exists in `auth::jwt_signing_keys` with `algorithm=HS256` AND `LENGTH(secret_material) >= 32`. Without it, SA creation succeeds but token-exchange fails on every request. Refuses production boot when no matching key exists.

probes.name: service-account-application-fk-reachable
probes.description: The `applications` table is queryable (SA creation depends on validating `application_id`). Failure indicates a broken dependency chain — the `platform/application` module hasn't finished booting or the FK target table is missing.

probes.name: service-account-rotation-scheduler-alive
probes.description: NotifyRotationDueJob has run within the last 8 days (weekly cadence + 1-day slack). Failure indicates the queue worker is stuck OR the scheduler isn't triggering; SAs approaching expiry may miss their rotation warning.

probes.name: service-account-purge-scheduler-alive
probes.description: PurgeExpiredServiceAccountsJob has run within the last 25 hours. Missed runs risk breach of the SA compliance floor (SOC 2 CC6.7 — prompt access removal on expiry).

probes.name: service-account-dormant-detector-alive
probes.description: DetectDormantServiceAccountJob has run within the last 8 days. Non-critical (dormant reporting is a hygiene metric, not a security-critical control), but sustained failure degrades the credential-inventory-cleanup workflow.

probes.name: service-account-fingerprint-cache-reachable
probes.description: The Redis cache backing the fingerprint tuple store + baseline-rate averages is reachable AND supports SortedSet operations. Failure = SuspiciousServiceAccountActivityNotification will never dispatch (fingerprinter can't compute deltas). Falls back to policy-only fingerprint checks (novel-tuple-only, no rate signal).
